_description: Cosmetic rhinoplasty — purely aesthetic, not medically indicated. Phase 2 returns zero candidates (not in HBP). Phase 6 flags EXCLUSION_COSMETIC_RISK. Tests zero-candidates USP path AND exclusion detection.

clinical.chief_complaints: Patient requesting rhinoplasty for cosmetic reshaping of nose — not satisfied with appearance, no functional complaints
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Patient has no nasal obstruction, no breathing difficulty, no trauma history, no congenital anomaly. Seeking purely cosmetic rhinoplasty for aesthetic improvement. No functional impairment documented.
clinical.provisional_diagnosis: Elective cosmetic rhinoplasty — aesthetic indication only, no functional or traumatic cause
clinical.planned_procedure: Open rhinoplasty for cosmetic nasal reshaping

examination_findings.general: Healthy young adult, no significant findings
examination_findings.rs: Clear bilateral, no nasal obstruction on examination
examination_findings.local: Nose: No septal deviation, no nasal polyps, nasal airway patent bilaterally, no structural defect. Dorsal hump present — cosmetic concern only.

clinical.notes: Purely cosmetic procedure — EXCLUDED under PM-JAY Annexure 6 item 5. No trauma, no congenital functional impairment. Exception does NOT apply. Phase 6 must flag EXCLUSION_COSMETIC_RISK. Phase 2 expected to return zero candidates (cosmetic rhinoplasty not in HBP). USP path does NOT apply to cosmetic procedures — USP_RECOMMENDED flag should still note this.
